summary.intent: Evolve Gossamer from a single-repo hardcoded SQLite setup to a multi-repo architecture where each repo has its own SQLite db, managed via a JSON config file at ~/.gossamer/config.json, with full UI support for onboarding repos, viewing repo statuses, and navigating checkpoints across repos.
summary.outcome: Multi-repo architecture implemented end-to-end: config.ts manages repo registry, each repo gets its own db via prisma db push on onboarding, indexer pollers iterate all repos, new Repos screen and repos-view toggle in ActiveSessions, BranchLog screen for git-log-based checkpoint navigation, CheckpointDetail rewritten to match SessionDetail's expanded view. Several cross-repo lookup bugs fixed via repoForCheckpoint() helper.

repo: The primary db is at ~/.claude/hook-handler.db, not a repo-local path — this is the real data source for the gossamer repo itself
The gossamer repo's localPath had a symlink discrepancy: /Users/stsoucas/gossamer vs /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer — requires realpathSync normalization
CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps git commit OIDs to checkpointIds — used for branch-log feature
Prisma 7 uses BetterSQLite3 driver adapter; schema can be applied to a new db with 'prisma db push --skip-generate' and DATABASE_URL env override
The entire/checkpoints/v1 branch is used as a git worktree for checkpoint indexing under .gossamer/checkpoints/
Shared rendering pipeline: logEventsToEvents → groupEvents → groupClaudeTurns → ClaudeTurnCard/RenderItem — exported from SessionDetail for reuse in BranchLog

code.finding: repoForCheckpoint() searches primary db first, then iterates all configured repo dbs — needed because repos view shows checkpoints from any db but detail routes only queried the primary db
POST /api/repos uses server's primary dbPath when localPath === repoDir to avoid creating an empty orphan db for the currently-served repo
Uses realpathSync to normalize localPath before comparing with config entries, preventing symlink-based mismatches
React synthetic event pooling: e.currentTarget is nulled before functional setState updater runs — must capture value synchronously: const v = e.currentTarget.value before setForm(f => ...)
ensureWorktree (calls mkdirSync) must be inside try/catch in indexCheckpointsForRepo — EACCES on repos without write permission would crash the entire server process

workflow: When adding per-repo indexing loops, use Promise.all and re-read config each tick so repos onboarded via UI are picked up without server restart
prisma db push --skip-generate is idempotent — safe to call on re-onboard; schema already applied is a no-op
For multi-db architectures with Prisma, pass dbPath via getDb(path) helper rather than using a singleton client
void-ing async calls in HTTP handlers allows immediate response while background work (worktree setup, initial indexing) continues

friction: Initial approach (Prisma Repo table with foreign key) was fully implemented then discarded in favor of JSON config — wasted schema migration work
Gossamer repo showed no data because config.json had wrong localPath (symlink vs real path) and wrong dbPath (empty gossamer.db vs real hook-handler.db)
404 on /api/checkpoints/:id/messages — route was never implemented; frontend was calling it; required removing the call and redesigning CheckpointDetail
404 on /api/v2/checkpoints/:id — checkpoint existed in a different repo's db; required repoForCheckpoint() multi-db search helper
EACCES crash on server startup when a configured repo path was not writable — ensureWorktree was outside try/catch
CheckpointDetail went through multiple rewrites: first stripped too bare (no summary), then restored with wrong structure, finally matched SessionDetail's expanded CheckpointRow
open_items: BranchLog feature was built but the original 404 on /api/checkpoints/:id/messages that triggered it was never cleanly resolved — CheckpointDetail now works but the relationship between BranchLog and CheckpointDetail navigation may need UX polish

The git fallback for latestCheckpointId (reading entire/checkpoints/v1 branch tree) was removed as pointless — if a repo has no db data there's nothing to show, but this means freshly onboarded repos with existing git checkpoint history won't surface until indexing runs
serve.ts realpathSync fix only applies to new server starts — existing config entries with wrong localPath paths must be manually corrected (as was done for gossamer)
pushSchema errors are non-fatal (logged as warnings) — a failed schema push leaves the repo without tables; there's no retry or user-visible error in the UI
The repos view latestCheckpointId comes from checkpointMetadata.findFirst ordered by id desc — this is insertion order, not chronological by commit date
